role_summary: Architectural domain specification defining the core domain of automated knowledge translation, outlining bounded contexts, subdomain responsibilities, unidirectional data flow, state persistence strategies, and architectural modeling gaps.
extracted_finding: The system centers on the core domain of Automated Knowledge Translation, partitioned into five bounded contexts with distinct DDD classifications: Repository Introspection & Curation (supporting artifact discovery and filtering), Semantic Extraction & Analysis (core technical-to-knowledge translation leveraging external AI), Information Aggregation & Synthesis (core terminology alignment and section composition), Pipeline Orchestration & Lifecycle Management (supporting stage execution and workspace governance), and External Intelligence Integration (generalized AI service abstraction). Data flows unidirectionally through a strict, stage-gated pipeline, with external intelligence acting as a cross-cutting capability within extraction. Intermediate analysis results are explicitly persisted to enable incremental processing, full transformation lineage auditability, and fault-tolerant stage resumption. Identified modeling gaps include undefined conflict resolution during synthesis, overlapping orchestration and workspace boundaries, and unspecified fallback mechanisms for external service degradation.
